source_policy: Use official platform documentation plus the local skill-creator schema bundled with the active Codex environment.

schema_sources.finding: A skill is a directory with SKILL.md plus optional scripts, references, assets, and agents metadata; SKILL.md requires name and description.
agents/openai.yaml supports interface display metadata, default_prompt, tool dependencies, and policy.allow_implicit_invocation.

tokenizer_sources.finding: OpenAI documents tiktoken as the practical tokenizer library for counting model tokens.
The local executable check used tiktoken 0.13.0 through uv.

tokenizer_result.command: uv run --with tiktoken python blcaptain-meta-skill/scripts/context_budget.py blcaptain-meta-skill/SKILL.md --tokenizer openai --encoding o200k_base
tokenizer_result.difference_note: The heuristic over-counted this SKILL.md by 242 tokens versus o200k_base; the heuristic remains useful as an offline conservative guard, but release checks should prefer tiktoken.

schema_sources.finding: Claude Agent Skills require SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter containing name and description, with optional supporting resources.
Claude token counting is exposed through Anthropic token counting APIs rather than a local tokenizer bundled with this repository.

schema_result.finding: The package keeps Claude-compatible name and description frontmatter and avoids platform-only metadata inside SKILL.md.

tokenizer_result.command_required: Use an authenticated Anthropic token counting request against the release-target Claude model.
tokenizer_result.release_gate: Public Claude release must add the API response with model, token_count, request shape, and date.

differences: Codex/OpenAI has local quick_validate and local openai.yaml guidance in the active skill-creator package.
Claude compatibility should be kept in SKILL.md/frontmatter/resource layout; Claude exact token count needs an authenticated API call.
The meta skill should keep platform-specific metadata outside SKILL.md unless the target platform explicitly supports it.
